description_long: This is a classic crescent-shaped croissant with a golden-brown, flaky exterior and a slightly uneven, hand-rolled appearance. The surface displays subtle ridges and folds, characteristic of laminated dough, with some areas showing more pronounced layering. The ends are tightly curled, while the central body is plump and slightly domed, suggesting a soft, airy interior. The color transitions from a light tan at the tips to a deeper golden hue along the main body, indicating a well-baked crust. There are no visible toppings or fillings, and the surface has a matte, slightly rough finish. The croissant appears fresh, with a delicate, breakable texture typical of traditional French pastries.
description: A golden-brown, flaky croissant with visible layers and a classic crescent shape, featuring a matte, slightly rough surface and no visible toppings.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full crescent shape, with both curled ends and the central dome clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view from one end highlights the tightly curled tip and the layered, rolled structure of the dough.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the broad, rounded back of the croissant, emphasizing the plumpness and even baking.
description_view_3: Side view from the opposite end shows the other curled tip and the subtle layering along the side.
